chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

text: 694 - وَقَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ تُشَدُّ اَلرِّحَالُ إِلاَّ إِلَى ثَلاَثَةِ مَسَاجِدَ اَلْمَسْجِدِ اَلْحَرَامِ وَ مَسْجِدِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ مَسْجِدِ اَلْكُوفَةِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.694 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Journeys should not be undertaken except to three mosques: the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram), the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (sw), and the Mosque of Kufa."
